title: CMS Energy Declares Quarterly Dividend on Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock
author: Prnewswire
description: CMS Energy's Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.2625 per depositary share on its 4.200% Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock, Series C. The dividend is payable on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of July 1, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is maintaining its dividend payments on preferred stock, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Regular dividend declarations are generally viewed positively as they indicate operational strength and investor confidence.

title: ATI Advisory Releases New Analysis of MSSP ACO Episodes, Highlighting Concentrated Financial Exposure and Opportunity Under LEAD
author: Ati Advisory
description: ATI Advisory analyzed 1.19 million MSSP shadow bundles worth $34.61 billion in spending, finding that Medicare ACO costs are concentrated in specific medical conditions and post-acute care settings. The analysis reveals high readmission rates and significant post-acute care utilization variation, presenting opportunities for ACOs to manage episode-level performance under CMS's new LEAD model with specialty integration and accountability mechanisms.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CMS's new LEAD model is presented as creating a pathway for ACOs to operationalize episode accountability and specialty integration, with the analysis supporting the model's framework as beneficial for managing healthcare costs and outcomes.

title: Consumers Energy, the Principal Subsidiary of CMS Energy, Declares Quarterly Dividend on Preferred Stock
author: Prnewswire
description: Consumers Energy, the principal subsidiary of CMS Energy, has declared a quarterly dividend on preferred stock of $1.125 per share on the $4.50 preferred stock, payable April 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 2, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial stability and commitment to returning value to shareholders. title: CMS Energy's Board of Directors Increases Quarterly Dividend on Common Stock to 57 Cents Per Share
author: Prnewswire
description: CMS Energy announced an increase in its quarterly dividend on common stock to 57 cents per share, up from 54.25 cents per share. The annualized dividend is now $2.28 per share, representing a 3.2% yield based on the stock's closing price of $71.60. The first quarter dividend is payable February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company increased its quarterly dividend by approximately 5% (from $0.5425 to $0.57 per share), demonstrating strong financial performance and commitment to returning value to shareholders. The CEO's statement reinforces confidence in the company's strategy execution.
